part.text:
You are working in an environment with SkyPilot installed.

Write a bash script at `/home/user/cleanup_clusters.sh` that performs a two-phase cluster cleanup using the SkyPilot CLI:

1. First, stop all running clusters (graceful shutdown that preserves disk state)
2. Then, terminate all clusters (permanent deletion including storage)

The script must:
- Run `sky status` and capture its output to `/home/user/cleanup.log` before making any changes
- Use `sky stop --all` to stop all clusters, appending output to `/home/user/cleanup.log`  
- Use `sky down --all -y` to terminate all clusters, appending output to `/home/user/cleanup.log`
- Be executable (chmod +x)

After writing the script, make it executable and run it. The script should exit with code 0 even if there are no clusters to clean up.

The log file `/home/user/cleanup.log` must exist after the script runs.

You are working in an environment with SkyPilot installed.

Write a bash script at `/home/user/cleanup_clusters.sh` that performs a two-phase cluster cleanup using the SkyPilot CLI:

1. First, stop all running clusters (graceful shutdown that preserves disk state)
2. Then, terminate all clusters (permanent deletion including storage)

The script must:
- Run `sky status` and capture its output to `/home/user/cleanup.log` before making any changes
- Use `sky stop --all` to stop all clusters, appending output to `/home/user/cleanup.log`  
- Use `sky down --all -y` to terminate all clusters, appending output to `/home/user/cleanup.log`
- Be executable (chmod +x)

After writing the script, make it executable and run it. The script should exit with code 0 even if there are no clusters to clean up.

The log file `/home/user/cleanup.log` must exist after the script runs.

input.content:
#!/bin/bash

# SkyPilot Cluster Cleanup Script
# This script performs a two-phase cleanup:
# 1. Stop all running clusters (graceful shutdown)
# 2. Terminate all clusters (permanent deletion)

LOG_FILE="/home/user/cleanup.log"

echo "========================================" | tee "$LOG_FILE"
echo "SkyPilot Cluster Cleanup - $(date)" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "========================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

echo "Phase 1: Checking cluster status..." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Running: sky status" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
sky status 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
STATUS_EXIT_CODE=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

echo "Phase 2: Stopping all running clusters..." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Running: sky stop --all" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
sky stop --all 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
STOP_EXIT_CODE=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

echo "Phase 3: Terminating all clusters..." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Running: sky down --all -y" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
sky down --all -y 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
DOWN_EXIT_CODE=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

echo "========================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Cleanup completed at $(date)" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "========================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# Exit with code 0 regardless of whether clusters existed or not
# The SkyPilot commands handle the case of no clusters gracefully
exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# SkyPilot Cluster Cleanup Script
# This script performs a two-phase cleanup:
# 1. Stop all running clusters (graceful shutdown)
# 2. Terminate all clusters (permanent deletion)

LOG_FILE="/home/user/cleanup.log"

echo "========================================" | tee "$LOG_FILE"
echo "SkyPilot Cluster Cleanup - $(date)" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "========================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

echo "Phase 1: Checking cluster status..." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Running: sky status" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
sky status 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
STATUS_EXIT_CODE=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

echo "Phase 2: Stopping all running clusters..." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Running: sky stop --all" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
sky stop --all 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
STOP_EXIT_CODE=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

echo "Phase 3: Terminating all clusters..." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Running: sky down --all -y" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
sky down --all -y 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
DOWN_EXIT_CODE=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

echo "========================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Cleanup completed at $(date)" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "========================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# Exit with code 0 regardless of whether clusters existed or not
# The SkyPilot commands handle the case of no clusters gracefully
exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# SkyPilot Cluster Cleanup Script
# This script performs a two-phase cleanup:
# 1. Stop all running clusters (graceful shutdown)
# 2. Terminate all clusters (permanent deletion)

LOG_FILE="/home/user/cleanup.log"

echo "========================================" | tee "$LOG_FILE"
echo "SkyPilot Cluster Cleanup - $(date)" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "========================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

echo "Phase 1: Checking cluster status..." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Running: sky status" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
sky status 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
STATUS_EXIT_CODE=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

echo "Phase 2: Stopping all running clusters..." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Running: sky stop --all" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
sky stop --all 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
STOP_EXIT_CODE=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

echo "Phase 3: Terminating all clusters..." | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Running: sky down --all -y" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
sky down --all -y 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
DOWN_EXIT_CODE=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

echo "========================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "Cleanup completed at $(date)" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
echo "========================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# Exit with code 0 regardless of whether clusters existed or not
# The SkyPilot commands handle the case of no clusters gracefully
exit 0
